Title: Landing Page
User Story: As a visitor, I want to see an attractive landing page, so that I understand what OPC Europe is and feel compelled to explore further.
Acceptance Criteria: Hero section with animated title, subtitle, and CTA buttons; How-it-works section with animated SVG connector lines; Featured tournaments section; Scroll-reveal animations on all content sections; Fully responsive at 1200px, 992px, and 640px breakpoints

Title: Tournament Listing Page
User Story: As a visitor, I want to browse a list of tournaments, so that I can find events I might want to attend.
Acceptance Criteria: Tournament cards with name, date, location, entry fee, and image; Filter by country and series; Sort by date and price; Responsive grid layout

Title: Rankings Page
User Story: As a visitor, I want to see player rankings, so that I can follow the competitive standings.
Acceptance Criteria: Leaderboard table with rank, name, nationality, and points; Responsive table layout; Hover states on rows

Title: Contact Page
User Story: As a visitor, I want to find contact information, so that I can reach out to the organizers.
Acceptance Criteria: Contact form layout; Email and social links; Responsive design

Title: Privacy Policy Page
User Story: As a visitor, I want to read the privacy policy, so that I understand how my data is handled.
Acceptance Criteria: Full privacy policy text content; Consistent header/footer; Readable typography

Title: Terms & Conditions Page
User Story: As a visitor, I want to read the terms and conditions, so that I understand the rules before participating.
Acceptance Criteria: Full terms text content; Consistent header/footer; Readable typography

Title: Responsible Gaming Page
User Story: As a visitor, I want to learn about responsible gaming practices, so that I can play safely.
Acceptance Criteria: Responsible gaming content and resources; Consistent header/footer; Readable typography

Title: Shared Design System (Static)
User Story: As a developer, I want a consistent design system across all static pages, so that the site looks polished and professional.
Acceptance Criteria: CSS custom properties for all colors, spacing, and typography; BEM-inspired class naming; Inter font (400-700 weights); Dark theme with #0c0e12 background and #1570ef accent; Reduced-motion media query for all animations

Title: Static Site Hosting Setup
User Story: As the product owner, I want the static site deployed to a reliable host, so that visitors can access it globally.
Acceptance Criteria: Site deployed and accessible at opc-europe.com; SSL certificate configured; All 8 pages loading correctly

Title: CDN & Asset Optimization
User Story: As a visitor, I want fast page loads, so that I have a smooth browsing experience.
Acceptance Criteria: Static assets served via CDN; Images optimized for web; Fast time-to-first-byte globally

Title: DNS & Domain Configuration
User Story: As the product owner, I want a custom domain configured, so that the site has a professional web address.
Acceptance Criteria: DNS records pointing to hosting provider; Domain opc-europe.com resolving correctly; WWW redirect configured

Title: Static Site Monitoring
User Story: As the product owner, I want basic uptime monitoring, so that I know if the site goes down.
Acceptance Criteria: Uptime monitoring configured; Alert notifications set up for downtime

Title: Vitest Unit Test Setup
User Story: As a developer, I want a unit testing framework, so that I can write fast isolated tests for components and utilities.
Acceptance Criteria: Vitest 4 configured with React Testing Library; jest-dom matchers available; Test scripts in package.json (test, test:watch); Path aliases working in test environment

Title: MSW API Mocking
User Story: As a developer, I want to mock Supabase API calls in tests, so that tests run without network dependencies.
Acceptance Criteria: MSW 2 server configured with Supabase handlers; Auth, tournament, registration, and profile endpoints mocked; Handlers reset between tests

Title: Test Data Factories
User Story: As a developer, I want reusable test data factories, so that I can quickly create test fixtures.
Acceptance Criteria: Factory functions for Profile, Tournament, and Registration; Sensible defaults with override capability; Used across all test files

Title: pgTAP Database Tests
User Story: As a developer, I want to test RLS policies and database functions directly, so that I can verify data security at the database layer.
Acceptance Criteria: pgTAP configured via supabase test db; Smoke test verifying setup; Test scripts in package.json

Title: Playwright E2E Setup
User Story: As a developer, I want end-to-end browser tests, so that I can verify complete user flows.
Acceptance Criteria: Playwright configured with test scripts; Base URL and browser settings configured; E2E test directory structure established

Title: Test Render Helpers
User Story: As a developer, I want shared render helpers, so that component tests have consistent provider wrappers.
Acceptance Criteria: renderWithProviders helper wrapping components with necessary context; Suspense boundary handling for useSearchParams; Cleanup between test renders

Title: Email/Password Signup
User Story: As a new user, I want to create an account with my email and password, so that I can access the platform.
Acceptance Criteria: Signup form with email and password fields; Password validation; Success redirects to verify-email page; Error messages for duplicate email or weak password; Profile auto-created via database trigger

Title: Email/Password Login
User Story: As a returning user, I want to log in with my email and password, so that I can access my account.
Acceptance Criteria: Login form with email and password fields; Success redirects to dashboard; Error message for invalid credentials; Session cookie set via @supabase/ssr

Title: Google OAuth Login
User Story: As a user, I want to sign in with Google, so that I can use my existing Google account.
Acceptance Criteria: Google OAuth button on login and signup pages; OAuth callback handler at /auth/callback; Profile auto-created on first OAuth login; Session established after callback

Title: Facebook OAuth Login
User Story: As a user, I want to sign in with Facebook, so that I can use my existing Facebook account.
Acceptance Criteria: Facebook OAuth button on login and signup pages; OAuth callback handler shared with Google; Profile auto-created on first login

Title: Email Verification
User Story: As a new user, I want to verify my email address, so that my account is confirmed.
Acceptance Criteria: Verification email sent on signup; Verify-email page shown after signup; Confirmation link activates account

Title: Session Management
User Story: As a logged-in user, I want my session to persist across page loads, so that I don't have to log in repeatedly.
Acceptance Criteria: Cookie-based sessions via @supabase/ssr; Session refreshed in middleware on every request; Expired sessions redirect to login

Title: Route Protection Middleware
User Story: As the product owner, I want routes protected by role, so that users can only access authorized pages.
Acceptance Criteria: Public routes accessible without auth; Protected routes redirect to /login if unauthenticated; Organizer routes require organizer role; Admin routes require admin role; Auth pages redirect to dashboard if already logged in

Title: Supabase Client Setup
User Story: As a developer, I want configured Supabase clients for all contexts, so that I can query the database from any layer.
Acceptance Criteria: Browser client for client components; Server client for server components with cookie access; Admin client with service role key (bypasses RLS); Middleware client for session refresh

Title: Database Profiles Table
User Story: As the system, I want user profiles auto-created on signup, so that every user has a complete profile record.
Acceptance Criteria: Profiles table with all user fields; Auto-create trigger on auth.users insert; RLS: public read, self-update, admin role update; Updated_at trigger for change tracking

Title: Role-Based Navigation
User Story: As a user, I want to see navigation relevant to my role, so that I can access the features available to me.
Acceptance Criteria: AppSidebar server component builds NavSection[] based on user role; Players see dashboard, tournaments, rankings; Organizers see additional organizer section; Admins see admin section; Sections have optional labels for grouping

Title: CSS Token Extraction
User Story: As a developer, I want shared design tokens between the static site and platform, so that both have a consistent look.
Acceptance Criteria: tokens.css extracted from site/styles.css; All color, spacing, typography, and shadow tokens as CSS custom properties; Imported via globals.css in the platform

Title: Inter Font Configuration
User Story: As a user, I want the platform to use the same font as the marketing site, so that the brand feels cohesive.
Acceptance Criteria: Inter font loaded via next/font/google (weights 400-700); CSS variable --font-inter applied to body; No layout shift from font loading

Title: Dark Theme Base Styles
User Story: As a user, I want the platform to match the dark premium theme of the marketing site, so that the experience feels unified.
Acceptance Criteria: Background #0c0e12 applied to body; Text colors using token variables; Card and surface colors matching site design; Scrollbar styled for dark theme

Title: Global Stylesheet
User Story: As a developer, I want a single entry-point stylesheet, so that all base styles and tokens are available throughout the platform.
Acceptance Criteria: globals.css imports tokens.css; Base element styles (body, headings, links, buttons); Utility classes for common patterns; Responsive helper classes

Title: Tournament Browse Page
User Story: As a player, I want to browse all available tournaments, so that I can find events to participate in.
Acceptance Criteria: Server component fetching all tournaments from Supabase; Tournament cards in a responsive grid; Shows name, dates, location, entry fee, image, capacity; Registration badges for logged-in users showing their status

Title: Tournament Country Filter
User Story: As a player, I want to filter tournaments by country, so that I can find events near me.
Acceptance Criteria: Dropdown populated with distinct countries from tournament data; URL-based filter parameter (shareable); Resets pagination when filter changes

Title: Tournament Series Filter
User Story: As a player, I want to filter tournaments by series, so that I can follow a specific tournament series.
Acceptance Criteria: Dropdown populated with distinct series from tournament data; URL-based filter parameter; Works in combination with country filter

Title: Tournament Sorting
User Story: As a player, I want to sort tournaments by date or price, so that I can find the most relevant events first.
Acceptance Criteria: Sort options: soonest first (default), latest first, cheapest first; URL-based sort parameter; Maintains active filters when sorting

Title: Tournament Pagination
User Story: As a player, I want paginated tournament results, so that the page loads quickly even with many tournaments.
Acceptance Criteria: 16 tournaments per page; URL-based page parameter (shareable); Previous/next navigation; Page count display; Maintains filters and sort when paging

Title: Tournament Detail Page
User Story: As a player, I want to see full details about a tournament, so that I can decide whether to register.
Acceptance Criteria: Full tournament info: name, description, dates, venue, entry fee, capacity; Organizer contact email; Registration count vs. capacity; Requires-verification notice if applicable; Dynamic page title via generateMetadata

Title: Tournament Registration
User Story: As a player, I want to register for a tournament, so that I can participate in the event.
Acceptance Criteria: Register button with 8 eligibility states (registered, not logged in, not onboarded, verification required, closed, deadline passed, full, eligible); Creates tournament_registration record with status registered; Requires onboarding_complete = true; Unique constraint prevents double registration

Title: Tournament Seed Data
User Story: As a developer, I want realistic seed data, so that the platform has content for testing and demos.
Acceptance Criteria: 16 seed tournaments across NL, DE, BE, GB; Varied dates, entry fees, and capacities; Mix of statuses (upcoming, in-progress, completed); Database migration for seed insertion

Title: Tournaments Database & RLS
User Story: As the system, I want tournament data secured with row-level security, so that only authorized users can modify tournaments.
Acceptance Criteria: Tournaments table with all required columns; RLS: public read for all users; Organizer/admin can create; Organizer can update own tournaments; Admin can delete any tournament

Title: Dashboard Stats Summary
User Story: As a player, I want to see my registration stats at a glance, so that I know my tournament activity.
Acceptance Criteria: Total tournaments registered count; Upcoming tournaments count; Stats displayed prominently at top of dashboard

Title: Upcoming Registrations Table
User Story: As a player, I want to see my upcoming tournament registrations, so that I can track what I've signed up for.
Acceptance Criteria: Table of registrations where tournament start_date is in the future; Columns: tournament name, date, location, status; Sorted by soonest first; Cancel button for each registration

Title: Past Registrations Table
User Story: As a player, I want to see my past tournament registrations, so that I can review my history.
Acceptance Criteria: Table of registrations where tournament start_date has passed; Columns: tournament name, date, location, status, placement (if results entered); Sorted by most recent first

Title: Cancel Registration
User Story: As a player, I want to cancel my tournament registration, so that I can free up my spot if plans change.
Acceptance Criteria: Two-step confirmation to prevent accidents; Updates registration status to cancelled; Sets cancelled_at timestamp; Removes from upcoming registrations; Refreshes dashboard data

Title: Registration Database & RLS
User Story: As the system, I want registration data secured with row-level security, so that players only see their own registrations.
Acceptance Criteria: Tournament_registrations table with status tracking; RLS: player sees own registrations; Organizer sees registrations for their tournaments; Admin sees all; Unique constraint on (tournament_id, player_id)

Title: Profile Edit Form
User Story: As a player, I want to edit my profile information, so that other players can learn about me.
Acceptance Criteria: Editable fields: display_name, city, home_country, nationality, bio; Form validation; Success feedback on save; Server action for profile update

Title: Avatar Upload
User Story: As a player, I want to upload a profile photo, so that I'm recognizable on the platform.
Acceptance Criteria: Image upload to Supabase Storage avatars bucket; Image preview before save; Accepts common image formats; Stored at {user_id}/avatar.{ext}; Public read access; User-scoped write/delete RLS

Title: Onboarding Completion Gate
User Story: As the system, I want to track onboarding completion, so that only players with complete profiles can register for tournaments.
Acceptance Criteria: onboarding_complete flag set to true on first profile save; Registration button shows Complete Profile First if not onboarded; Links to /profile for completion

Title: Profile Slug Generation
User Story: As a player, I want a clean URL for my public profile, so that I can share it easily.
Acceptance Criteria: Auto-generated slug from display_name via generate_profile_slug() function; Unique with collision handling (-2, -3 suffixes); Updated when display_name changes; Used in /players/[slug] routes

Title: Social Links
User Story: As a player, I want to add social media links to my profile, so that others can connect with me.
Acceptance Criteria: Social links JSON field on profile; Editable in profile form; Displayed on public player profile

Title: Avatar Storage Bucket
User Story: As the system, I want a secure storage bucket for avatars, so that images are stored safely with proper access control.
Acceptance Criteria: Supabase Storage avatars bucket created; Public read policy for all avatars; Write/delete restricted to file owner via RLS; Path structure: {user_id}/avatar.{ext}

Title: Organizer Dashboard
User Story: As an organizer, I want a dashboard showing my tournaments, so that I can manage my events from one place.
Acceptance Criteria: List of organizer's tournaments grouped by status (upcoming, in-progress, completed); Quick stats: total registrations, tournaments managed; Create new tournament button; Only shows tournaments where organizer_id matches current user

Title: Create Tournament
User Story: As an organizer, I want to create a new tournament, so that players can discover and register for my event.
Acceptance Criteria: Tournament form with all required fields (name, dates, venue, capacity, entry fee, description); Optional fields: series, image_url, points_multiplier, requires_verification; Validation for required fields and date logic; Server action creates record with organizer_id set to current user

Title: Edit Tournament
User Story: As an organizer, I want to edit my tournament details, so that I can update information as plans change.
Acceptance Criteria: Pre-populated form with existing tournament data; Toggle registration_open on/off; Toggle requires_verification; Set/update registration_deadline; Only tournament owner or admin can edit; Server action updates record

Title: View Registrations
User Story: As an organizer, I want to see who has registered for my tournament, so that I can manage participants.
Acceptance Criteria: Table of all registered players; Columns: name, nationality, registration date, status; Registration count vs. capacity display; Sortable columns

Title: Update Registration Status
User Story: As an organizer, I want to update a player's registration status, so that I can confirm attendance or mark no-shows.
Acceptance Criteria: Status dropdown per registration row (registered, confirmed, cancelled, no_show); Immediate save on status change; Server action for status update; Only tournament organizer or admin can update

Title: Export Registrations CSV
User Story: As an organizer, I want to export registrations as CSV, so that I can use the data in external tools.
Acceptance Criteria: Export button generates CSV file; Includes all registration columns; Downloads immediately in browser; Formatted for spreadsheet compatibility

Title: Tournament Form Component
User Story: As a developer, I want a reusable tournament form, so that create and edit share the same validated UI.
Acceptance Criteria: TournamentForm component used by both create and edit pages; Field validation; Date picker inputs; Conditional fields based on tournament state

Title: Organizer Route Protection
User Story: As the system, I want organizer routes protected, so that only users with the organizer role can access management tools.
Acceptance Criteria: All /organizer/* routes require organizer role; Middleware redirects unauthorized users; RLS policies enforce organizer ownership on data mutations

Title: Results Entry Page
User Story: As an organizer, I want to enter tournament results, so that player stats and rankings are updated.
Acceptance Criteria: Select players from confirmed registrations; Enter placement for each player (1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc.); Points auto-calculated and displayed; Bulk entry mode for efficiency; Save button persists all results

Title: Points Calculation Engine
User Story: As the system, I want points calculated automatically from placements, so that rankings stay accurate.
Acceptance Criteria: calculate_points() Postgres function using configurable brackets; Base points multiplied by tournament.points_multiplier; Default brackets: 1st=1000, 2nd=750, 3rd=500, 4th=400, 5th=350, 6-10=300, 11-20=200, 21-50=100, 51+=50; Client-side points.ts for preview calculation

Title: Auto-Compute Player Stats
User Story: As the system, I want player stats recomputed automatically after results are entered, so that rankings are always current.
Acceptance Criteria: Postgres trigger on tournament_results insert/update; compute_player_stats() recalculates: total_points, tournament_count, win_count, top3_count, avg_finish, best_finish; Updates current_rank based on total_points DESC; Stores previous_rank for change tracking

Title: Achievement System
User Story: As a player, I want to earn achievements for milestones, so that I feel rewarded for my accomplishments.
Acceptance Criteria: 8 achievement definitions seeded in achievements table; check_achievements() function auto-awards: First Blood (1 tournament), Regular (5), Veteran (20), Champion (1 win), Triple Crown (3 wins), Podium Master (10 top-3), Point Machine (10k points), Globetrotter (3+ countries); Triggered automatically after stats recomputation; player_achievements junction table with unique constraint

Title: Results Database & RLS
User Story: As the system, I want results data secured with row-level security, so that only authorized users can enter results.
Acceptance Criteria: tournament_results table with placement and points_awarded; RLS: public read for all; Organizer insert/update for own tournaments; Admin full access; Unique constraint on (tournament_id, player_id)

Title: Player Stats Table
User Story: As the system, I want a materialized stats table, so that rankings queries are fast.
Acceptance Criteria: player_stats table with aggregated fields; RLS: public read; Function-only writes (no direct user updates); last_computed timestamp for tracking freshness

Title: Results Server Action
User Story: As a developer, I want a server action for saving results, so that the results page can submit data securely.
Acceptance Criteria: saveResults() server action in lib/actions/results.ts; Accepts tournament ID and array of {player_id, placement}; Validates organizer ownership; Upserts tournament_results records; Triggers auto-compute via database triggers

Title: Save Results Confirmation
User Story: As an organizer, I want confirmation after saving results, so that I know the data was persisted successfully.
Acceptance Criteria: Success toast/message after save; Error handling for failed saves; Redirect or refresh after successful entry

Title: Country Configuration Table
User Story: As the system, I want configurable per-country settings, so that points can vary by country.
Acceptance Criteria: country_config table with country_code, country_name, global_multiplier, custom_brackets; 15 European countries seeded (NL, DE, BE, GB, FR, ES, IT, PT, AT, CZ, PL, CH, IE, DK, SE); Auto-insert trigger for new countries appearing in tournament data; RLS: public read, admin manage

Title: Default Points Brackets Admin
User Story: As an admin, I want to edit the default points brackets, so that I can adjust the scoring system.
Acceptance Criteria: Editable table showing placement ranges and base points; 9 default brackets seeded; Save button persists changes via updateDefaultBrackets() server action; Changes affect future points calculations

Title: Country Multiplier Admin
User Story: As an admin, I want to set per-country point multipliers, so that different regions can weight differently.
Acceptance Criteria: Countries table with inline multiplier editing; Expandable custom brackets per country; updateCountryConfig() server action; Changes affect future points calculations

Title: Per-Country Player Stats
User Story: As the system, I want per-country player rankings, so that players can see how they rank within a specific country.
Acceptance Criteria: player_country_stats table with composite PK (player_id, country_code); Same stat fields as global player_stats; compute_player_country_stats() function; Called by updated compute_player_stats(); RLS: public read

Title: Weighted Global Rankings
User Story: As the system, I want global rankings weighted by country multipliers, so that the overall leaderboard reflects the configured scoring.
Acceptance Criteria: Updated compute_player_stats() uses country multipliers; Global total = SUM(country_stats * country_config.global_multiplier); Both global and per-country rankings updated on recompute

Title: Recompute All Stats
User Story: As an admin, I want to trigger a full stats recomputation, so that I can apply configuration changes retroactively.
Acceptance Criteria: recomputeAllStats() server action; Calls compute_all_player_stats() via Supabase RPC; Updates all player stats, country stats, and rankings in one operation

Title: Public Leaderboard Page
User Story: As a visitor, I want to view player rankings, so that I can see who the top players are.
Acceptance Criteria: Fetch from player_stats ordered by total_points DESC; Columns: rank, avatar, name, nationality, points, tournaments played; Rank change indicators (up/down/same arrows) via current_rank vs previous_rank; Top player visually highlighted; 20 rows per page with pagination

Title: Leaderboard Search
User Story: As a visitor, I want to search for a player on the leaderboard, so that I can find a specific person's ranking.
Acceptance Criteria: Search input filters by player name; Server-side ilike() query; Results update on search; Maintains other active filters

Title: Leaderboard Country Filter
User Story: As a visitor, I want to filter rankings by country, so that I can see regional standings.
Acceptance Criteria: Country dropdown filter; Server-side filtering; URL-based parameter for sharing; Works with search filter

Title: Public Player Profile Page
User Story: As a visitor, I want to view a player's profile, so that I can see their stats and tournament history.
Acceptance Criteria: SEO-friendly /players/[slug] URL; Player header: avatar, display name, nationality, member since, global rank; Responsive layout

Title: Player Stats Grid
User Story: As a visitor, I want to see a player's statistics, so that I can understand their performance.
Acceptance Criteria: Stats grid showing: total points, tournaments played, wins, top-3 finishes, avg finish, best finish; Responsive grid layout; Visual styling consistent with design system

Title: Achievement Badges Display
User Story: As a visitor, I want to see a player's achievements, so that I can appreciate their accomplishments.
Acceptance Criteria: All 8 achievements displayed as badges; Earned achievements highlighted with color; Unearned achievements greyed out; Achievement name and description on hover/display

Title: Tournament History Table
User Story: As a visitor, I want to see a player's tournament history, so that I can review their past performances.
Acceptance Criteria: Paginated table of tournament results; Columns: tournament name, date, country, placement, points; Sorted by most recent first; Pagination for players with many results

Title: Rank Badge Component
User Story: As a developer, I want a reusable rank badge, so that rank is displayed consistently.
Acceptance Criteria: RankBadge component with visual styling for top-3 (gold, silver, bronze); Standard styling for other ranks; Used on leaderboard and player profiles

Title: Verification Session Creation API
User Story: As the system, I want to create Didit verification sessions, so that players can start identity verification.
Acceptance Criteria: POST /api/verification/create-session endpoint; Authenticates user; Checks if already verified; Calls Didit v3 API with x-api-key header; Stores didit_session_id on profile; Returns verification URL for redirect

Title: Didit Webhook Handler
User Story: As the system, I want to process Didit verification results, so that verified players are updated automatically.
Acceptance Criteria: POST /api/webhooks/didit endpoint; Validates HMAC-SHA256 signature via x-signature-simple header; Only processes status=Approved webhooks; Sets identity_verified=true and identity_verified_at; Extracts date_of_birth from verification data

Title: Verify Identity Page
User Story: As a player, I want to verify my identity, so that I can register for tournaments that require verification.
Acceptance Criteria: Verify identity page at /verify-identity; Creates verification session via API; Redirects to Didit's hosted verification page; User completes ID upload and selfie on Didit; No client SDK dependency (redirect-based flow)

Title: Verification Status Badge
User Story: As a player, I want to see my verification status, so that I know whether I need to verify.
Acceptance Criteria: VerificationStatus component showing verified/unverified badge; Displayed on profile page (always) and dashboard (when unverified); Links to /verify-identity when not yet verified; Green badge when verified

Title: Didit Utility Library
User Story: As a developer, I want shared Didit utilities, so that API calls and webhook validation are centralized.
Acceptance Criteria: lib/didit.ts with createVerificationSession() and validateWebhookSignature(); Environment variable configuration (DIDIT_API_KEY, DIDIT_WEBHOOK_SECRET, NEXT_PUBLIC_DIDIT_WORKFLOW_ID); Reusable across API routes

Title: Admin Dashboard
User Story: As an admin, I want an overview dashboard, so that I can monitor platform health at a glance.
Acceptance Criteria: Overview stats: total users, verified users, total tournaments, active tournaments; Recent activity feed showing new signups, registrations, and results; Admin-only access enforced by middleware and RLS

Title: User Management Page
User Story: As an admin, I want to manage users, so that I can handle support and moderation tasks.
Acceptance Criteria: Table of all users with search and filter capabilities; Columns: name, email, role, verified status, signup date; Actions: view profile, change role; Searchable by name or email

Title: Organizer Invitation System
User Story: As an admin, I want to invite organizers by email, so that approved event managers get the right access on signup.
Acceptance Criteria: Invite form: enter email to create organizer_invitations record; List of current organizers and pending invitations; Database trigger auto-promotes matching email to organizer role on signup; Records accepted_at timestamp when claimed

Title: Tournament Oversight Page
User Story: As an admin, I want to see all tournaments across organizers, so that I can oversee the entire platform.
Acceptance Criteria: Table of all tournaments from all organizers; Filter by status, organizer, date range; Admin can cancel any tournament via cancelTournamentAdmin() server action; Full tournament details accessible

Title: Admin Server Actions
User Story: As a developer, I want admin-specific server actions, so that admin operations are secure and centralized.
Acceptance Criteria: promoteToOrganizer(userId) updates user role; inviteOrganizer(email, invitedBy) creates invitation; cancelTournamentAdmin(tournamentId) for admin cancellation; requireAdmin() helper validates admin role before execution

Title: Organizer Invitations Database
User Story: As the system, I want invitation records persisted, so that the auto-promote trigger works reliably.
Acceptance Criteria: organizer_invitations table with id, email (unique), invited_by, created_at, accepted_at; RLS: admin only; handle_organizer_invitation() trigger on profiles INSERT; Auto-sets role=organizer if email matches invitation

Title: Admin Route Protection
User Story: As the system, I want admin routes strictly protected, so that only admins can access admin features.
Acceptance Criteria: All /admin/* routes require role=admin; Middleware enforces role check; Unauthorized users redirected; RLS policies enforce admin-only on data mutations

Title: Supabase Project Setup
User Story: As a developer, I want a configured Supabase project, so that the platform has a production database and auth provider.
Acceptance Criteria: Supabase project created (ref: uxlnhyeijfeiurwleecy); Auth providers configured (email, Google, Facebook); Database migrations applied (001 through 013); Storage bucket created; RLS policies active on all tables

Title: Environment Configuration
User Story: As a developer, I want environment variables properly configured, so that all services connect correctly in each environment.
Acceptance Criteria: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and ANON_KEY set; SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY for admin client; DIDIT_API_KEY and DIDIT_WEBHOOK_SECRET; NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL for callbacks; OAuth redirect URIs configured in Supabase dashboard

Title: Platform Hosting Deployment
User Story: As the product owner, I want the platform deployed to production, so that users can access the application.
Acceptance Criteria: Next.js app deployed to hosting provider; Custom subdomain app.opc-europe.com configured; SSL certificate active; Build and deployment pipeline working

Title: OAuth Redirect Configuration
User Story: As the system, I want OAuth redirects configured for production, so that social login works on the live site.
Acceptance Criteria: Google OAuth redirect URI pointing to production /auth/callback; Facebook OAuth redirect URI configured; Supabase auth settings updated with production URLs

Title: Webhook Endpoint Configuration
User Story: As the system, I want webhook endpoints configured for production, so that Didit can notify the platform of verification results.
Acceptance Criteria: Didit webhook URL pointing to production /api/webhooks/didit; HMAC secret configured in both Didit dashboard and platform env vars; Webhook accessible from external services
